engine.ladder_reasoning: Sells a defined expertise (strategic Squarespace design service) — the whole spine is credentials, team, process/Design Sprint, testimonials and celebrity logo wall persuading you to trust the firm. Portfolio is heavy but supports the trust argument rather than being the sole proof-of-sale.

goal.one_action: Book a free discovery call to hire the design team (secondary: buy a $299 template)

structure.archetype_note: Agency site with a dual monetization: high-ticket done-for-you service (book-a-call) AND a self-serve template store with cart/checkout ($299 x 40+). Two conversion jobs, not one — hence out of ICP.

mapped.observed_as: It's Time Your Website Caught Up With Your Reputation

unmapped.observed: Shop Our Premium Squarespace Website Templates (grid of 25+ purchasable templates at $299, cart/checkout)
unmapped.why_no_fit: A transactable product store grid with checkout is not a persuasion section — it's a catalog/commerce surface the section list has no id for

unmapped.observed: Match Me to a Template / TAKE THE QUIZ (typeform product-recommender)
unmapped.why_no_fit: An interactive recommendation quiz that routes visitors to a matching product is neither a lead-form nor a static section

proposed_new.name: product-finder-quiz
proposed_new.evidence: "TAKE THE QUIZ" / "Match Me to a Template" CTA opens a typeform quiz that recommends a matching template — an interactive product-matching flow, distinct from a static lead-form or catalog grid.

components_needed: product catalog grid with price + buy

confidence.why: fetch_quality=full; all 5 pages captured with ordered sections, CTAs, forms, embeds, commerce and proof counts.

gap_findings: Site combines two conversion jobs (high-ticket book-a-call service + self-serve $299 template store with checkout) — the one-action model and single-engine pick cannot express a dual-monetization agency+store hybrid.
A template SHOP with real cart/checkout is beyond the closed 'catalog' vocab (catalog implies browse; here it transacts) — commerce/checkout has no section id or in-ICP handling.
'Become an Affiliate' recruits affiliates INBOUND (an affiliate-program signup) — distinct from registry's affiliate-hub (outbound curated links); not captured as a capability.
